title: Google Just Launched a $1 Billion AI Price War — Here's What It Means for $GOOGL
subtitle: Alphabet slashes Gemini API pricing up to 80%, triggering a race to the bottom that could reshape the entire AI stack.
summary: Google launched a sweeping AI pricing overhaul, cutting Gemini API costs by up to 80% and giving away $1B in cloud credits — a move that crushes margins for competitors while cementing Google's AI infrastructure moat.

image.caption: Google AI pricing overhaul reshapes the cloud AI market

bodyHtml:
Google just dropped a bomb on the AI industry — and it's not a model launch.

Alphabet slashed Gemini API pricing by up to 80%, extended $1 billion in cloud credits to startups, and made its most powerful models cheaper than OpenAI's by a wide margin. This isn't a promotion. It's a strategy.

Here's the play no one's talking about: Google doesn't need to make money on AI inference. They need to make AI addictive. The more companies build on Gemini, the more compute flows through Google Cloud's TPU infrastructure — and that's where the real margin lives.

Think AWS in 2010. Amazon slashed prices, crushed competitors, and locked in a generation of startups. Google is running the same playbook for the AI era.

The numbers back it up. GOOGL sits at $388 with a $4.71 trillion market cap. Revenue grew 21.8% to $422.5B with net income of $160.2B. Cloud revenue alone surged 35% — and that's before this pricing move triggers adoption acceleration.

For investors, this is a two-sided coin. Short-term? AI startups that built on OpenAI API just got a massive incentive to switch. That's a headwind for Microsoft's Azure AI business. Long-term? Google is commoditizing the AI inference layer — and whoever controls the infrastructure wins.

The AI price war is here. Google came armed with a $2.1 trillion balance sheet and a decade of chip design moat. Good luck competing with that.

videos.title: Google's AI endgame is here… everything you missed at I/O 2026
